features.details: Review the existing sidebar component structure, CSS styles, and identify the files that need modification. Document current responsive behavior if any exists.
Create or add a hamburger menu button that appears only on mobile viewports. This button will trigger the sidebar expand/collapse functionality.
Add state management for tracking sidebar visibility on mobile. Implement toggle functionality that shows/hides the sidebar when the toggle button is clicked.

features.description: Add responsive CSS styles for mobile sidebar
features.details: Implement CSS media queries to handle sidebar display on mobile devices. Sidebar should be hidden by default on mobile and appear as an overlay when toggled.
Implement CSS transitions for smooth sidebar expand/collapse animations. Include slide-in/slide-out effects and backdrop overlay fade.

features.description: Add backdrop overlay for mobile sidebar
features.details: Implement a semi-transparent backdrop overlay that appears when the sidebar is expanded on mobile. Clicking the backdrop should close the sidebar.

features.description: Handle body scroll lock when sidebar is open
features.details: Prevent body scroll when the sidebar is expanded on mobile to avoid dual-scroll issues and improve user experience.

features.description: Add unit tests for sidebar toggle functionality
features.details: Write unit tests to verify sidebar expand/collapse behavior, state management, and responsive class application.

features.description: Test on various mobile screen sizes
features.details: Manually test the sidebar functionality on different mobile screen sizes and orientations. Verify touch interactions and animation smoothness.

overall_approach: Implement a responsive sidebar that collapses by default on mobile devices and can be toggled via a hamburger menu button. This involves adding mobile-specific CSS styles, a toggle button component, state management for the sidebar visibility, and smooth transition animations.
potential_risks: Existing desktop layout may be affected if responsive breakpoints are not properly defined
Z-index conflicts with other UI elements when sidebar is expanded on mobile
Touch interaction issues on mobile devices
Performance impact from animation on lower-end mobile devices
